tldr: Cat Ferguson (Movistar) won the sprint into Vila-Real ahead of Letizia Paternoster and Letizia Borghesi after the early climbs gave way to a long flat run-in. Demi Vollering finished safely in the front group and kept the overall lead.

narrative.headline: Ferguson takes the sprint in Vila-Real as Vollering holds the lead
narrative.body:
The 115.5 km circuit around Vila-Real packed its climbing into the first half before roughly 50 km of mostly downhill and flat roads back to the line — a profile that pointed to a sprint if the fast finishers survived the hills. They did, and the day came down to a group gallop.

Movistar's 19-year-old Cat Ferguson timed the finish best, winning ahead of Letizia Paternoster (Liv AlUla Jayco) and Letizia Borghesi (AG Insurance-Soudal). The tight time spread among the leaders confirmed a bunch finish rather than a successful long break. Demi Vollering came in safely with the front of the race and retained the overall lead heading into the scheduled queen stage.

gcImpact.headline: No change at the top — Vollering still leads
gcImpact.body: Ferguson's stage win came with the time bonus but from well down on GC, so the overall order at the front was unchanged: Vollering led from Squiban (+0:56) and Chladoňová (+0:58), with Chladoňová still in the youth jersey. The flat finish kept the GC contenders together and left the race to be settled on the mountains — or so it seemed before the weather intervened.

riderPerformances.name: Cat Ferguson
riderPerformances.narrative: Movistar's young all-rounder survived the first-half climbs and produced the fastest finish into Vila-Real, beating Paternoster and Borghesi for her first win of the 2026 season. A statement sprint at just 19, and Movistar's stage of the race.

keyMoments.what: Won the reduced bunch sprint in Vila-Real ahead of Paternoster and Borghesi

riderPerformances.name: Demi Vollering
riderPerformances.narrative: Rode a controlled defensive day, staying out of trouble in the front group on the flat run-in to Vila-Real and keeping the leader's jersey intact ahead of the scheduled summit-finish queen stage.

keyMoments.what: Finished safely in the front group to retain the overall lead
